steps.title: Use the square diagonal
steps.content: For a square inscribed in a circle, the diagonal of the square is the diameter of the circle. This works because the diagonal connects two opposite vertices on the circle and passes through the center.

steps.title: Find the diameter
steps.content:
The diameter is twice the radius, so double the given radius:
$$2\cdot\frac{18\sqrt{2}}{2}=18\sqrt{2}$$

steps.title: Solve for the side length
steps.content:
If the side length is $s$, the square's diagonal is $s\sqrt{2}$ by the Pythagorean theorem on two sides of the square. Set that equal to the circle's diameter:
$$s\sqrt{2}=18\sqrt{2}$$
$$s=18$$
The side length is $18$ inches. The answer is A.
